---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Qingyang Du"
date: "2026-02-27"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}

###############################################################################
#                   REPORT TABLES SCRIPT (kableExtra)
#
# Prereq: Run the following scripts first:
#   01_data_cleaning_v3.R      → hiv_cleaned.RData (dat_cc)
#   Frequentist-code.Rmd       → freq_results.RData
#   Bayesian.Rmd               → bayes_results.RData
#
# Saved objects loaded:
#   freq_results.RData:
#     results_list      — named list of lm model objects
#     drug_effects      — tibble (Outcome, Model, estimate, std.error,
#                           statistic, p.value, conf.low, conf.high)
#     mediation_compare — tibble (Outcome, est_adj, est_adj_adh,
#                           change, pct_change)
#     r2_table          — tibble of R² values across 3 model tiers
#
#   bayes_results.RData:
#     bayes_results       — named list of fit objects, summaries, prep data
#     bayes_drug_effects  — tibble (Outcome, Model, Estimate, Posterior_SD,
#                             HPDI_2.5, HPDI_97.5, ESS, MCSE,
#                             MCSE_pct_SD, MCSE_OK)
#     posterior_probs     — tibble (Outcome, P_direction,
#                             Clinical_threshold, P_clinically_meaningful,
#                             Threshold_label)
#     bayes_mediation     — tibble (Outcome, est_adj, est_adj_adh,
#                             change, pct_change)
#     fit_comparison      — tibble of DIC, WAIC, LOOIC per model
#
# Produces publication-ready tables for the final report:
#   Table 2:  Frequentist — hard drug use effect (Adjusted models × 4 outcomes)
#   Table 2b: Frequentist — all covariate effects (Adjusted model × 4 outcomes)
#   Table 3:  Bayesian — posterior summary (2 models × 4 outcomes)
#   Table 3b: Bayesian — all covariate effects (Adjusted model × 4 outcomes)
#   Table 4:  Frequentist vs. Bayesian comparison (adjusted model)
#   Table 5:  Mediation assessment (both frameworks)
#
# Note: Table 1 (baseline characteristics) is produced in
#       03_table1_kableExtra.R
###############################################################################

library(tidyverse)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(broom)

load("freq_results.RData")
load("bayes_results.RData")


# =============================================================================
# SHARED: Outcome display order
# =============================================================================

outcome_order <- c("Change in Log10 Viral Load",
                   "Change in CD4+ T Cell Count",
                   "Change in Physical QoL",
                   "Change in Mental QoL")

# Formatting helpers
fmt_est_ci <- function(est, lo, hi, digits = 3) {
  paste0(formatC(est, format = "f", digits = digits),
         " (",
         formatC(lo, format = "f", digits = digits),
         ", ",
         formatC(hi, format = "f", digits = digits),
         ")")
}

fmt_pval <- function(p) {
  ifelse(p < 0.001, "<0.001", formatC(p, format = "f", digits = 3))
}


# =============================================================================
# TABLE 2:  FREQUENTIST RESULTS — HARD DRUG USE EFFECT (Adjusted Models Only)
# =============================================================================
# Source: drug_effects from freq_results.RData
# Rows:  4 outcomes
# Cols:  Adjusted, Adjusted + Adherence
# Cells: Estimate (95% CI), p-value

freq_table <- drug_effects %>%
  filter(Model != "Unadjusted") %>%
  mutate(
    cell = paste0(
      fmt_est_ci(estimate, conf.low, conf.high),
      ", p=",
      fmt_pval(p.value)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(Outcome, Model, cell) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Model, values_from = cell) %>%
  mutate(Outcome = factor(Outcome, levels = outcome_order)) %>%
  arrange(Outcome) %>%
  rename(
    " "                    = Outcome,
    "Adjusted"             = Adjusted,
    "Adjusted + Adherence" = `Adjusted + Adherence`
  )

table2_kbl <- kbl(freq_table,
                  format  = "html",
                  align   = c("l", "c", "c"),
                  caption = paste0("Table 2. Estimated Effect of Hard Drug Use on ",
                                   "Change in Treatment Outcomes (Frequentist)"),
                  escape  = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE,
                font_size  = 13,
                position   = "center") %>%
  footnote(general = paste0(
             "Values shown as estimate (95% CI), p-value. ",
             "Outcomes are change scores (Year 2 minus Baseline). ",
             "Adjusted: change ~ hard drug use + age + race + education + BMI + smoking. ",
             "Adjusted + Adherence: Adjusted model + ART adherence category. ",
             "Viral load modeled on the log10 scale. ",
             "Reference group: no hard drug use."),
           general_title = "Note: ",
           footnote_as_chunk = TRUE)

table2_kbl


# =============================================================================
# TABLE 2b: FREQUENTIST — ALL COVARIATE EFFECTS (Adjusted Model)
# =============================================================================
# Full coefficient table for the adjusted model across all 4 outcomes.
# Source: results_list from freq_results.RData

# Readable variable labels
var_labels <- c(
  "(Intercept)"            = "Intercept",
  "hard_drugs_blYes"       = "Hard Drug Use (Yes vs. No)",
  "age_bl"                 = "Age",
  "RACE_bin_blOther"       = "Race (Other vs. Non-Hispanic White)",
  "EDUC_bin_blCollege degree+" = "Education (College+ vs. Below)",
  "BMI_bl"                 = "BMI",
  "SMOKE_bin_blCurrent"    = "Smoking (Current vs. Non/Former)"
)

freq_covar_table <- map_dfr(names(results_list), function(nm) {
  r <- results_list[[nm]]
  tidy(r$adj, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
    mutate(Outcome = r$label)
}) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable = var_labels[term],
    Variable = ifelse(is.na(Variable), term, Variable),
    cell     = paste0(
      fmt_est_ci(estimate, conf.low, conf.high),
      ", p=",
      fmt_pval(p.value)
    ),
    Outcome = factor(Outcome, levels = outcome_order)
  ) %>%
  select(Outcome, Variable, cell) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Outcome, values_from = cell)

# Enforce variable display order (intercept first, then exposure, then confounders)
var_display_order <- c("Intercept",
                       "Hard Drug Use (Yes vs. No)",
                       "Age",
                       "Race (Other vs. Non-Hispanic White)",
                       "Education (College+ vs. Below)",
                       "BMI",
                       "Smoking (Current vs. Non/Former)")

freq_covar_table <- freq_covar_table %>%
  mutate(Variable = factor(Variable, levels = var_display_order)) %>%
  arrange(Variable) %>%
  rename(" " = Variable)

table2b_kbl <- kbl(freq_covar_table,
                   format  = "html",
                   align   = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c"),
                   caption = paste0("Table 2b. Frequentist Adjusted Model: ",
                                    "All Covariate Effects Across Outcomes"),
                   escape  = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE,
                font_size  = 12,
                position   = "center") %>%
  footnote(general = paste0(
             "Values shown as estimate (95% CI), p-value. ",
             "Model: change ~ hard drug use + age + race + education + BMI + smoking. ",
             "Outcomes are change scores (Year 2 minus Baseline). ",
             "Viral load modeled on the log10 scale."),
           general_title = "Note: ",
           footnote_as_chunk = TRUE)

table2b_kbl


# =============================================================================
# TABLE 3:  BAYESIAN RESULTS — POSTERIOR SUMMARY FOR HARD DRUG USE
# =============================================================================
# Source: bayes_drug_effects + posterior_probs from bayes_results.RData
# Rows:  4 outcomes × 2 models
# Cols:  Posterior Mean (95% HPDI), P(direction), P(clinically meaningful)

# Merge posterior probabilities (from adjusted model) into drug effects
bayes_tbl <- bayes_drug_effects %>%
  left_join(
    posterior_probs %>%
      select(Outcome, P_direction, P_clinically_meaningful, Threshold_label),
    by = "Outcome"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Mean_HPDI = fmt_est_ci(Estimate, HPDI_2.5, HPDI_97.5),
    P_dir_display    = formatC(P_direction, format = "f", digits = 3),
    P_clin_display   = formatC(P_clinically_meaningful, format = "f", digits = 3),
    Outcome = factor(Outcome, levels = outcome_order)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Outcome, Model) %>%
  select(Outcome, Model, Mean_HPDI, P_dir_display, P_clin_display) %>%
  rename(
    " "                           = Outcome,
    "Model"                       = Model,
    "Posterior Mean (95% HPDI)"   = Mean_HPDI,
    "P(Direction)"                = P_dir_display,
    "P(Clinically Meaningful)"    = P_clin_display
  )

table3_kbl <- kbl(bayes_tbl,
                  format  = "html",
                  align   = c("l", "l", "c", "c", "c"),
                  caption = paste0("Table 3. Estimated Effect of Hard Drug Use on ",
                                   "Change in Treatment Outcomes (Bayesian)"),
                  escape  = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE,
                font_size  = 13,
                position   = "center") %>%
  pack_rows(outcome_order[1], 1, 2, bold = TRUE, italic = TRUE) %>%
  pack_rows(outcome_order[2], 3, 4, bold = TRUE, italic = TRUE) %>%
  pack_rows(outcome_order[3], 5, 6, bold = TRUE, italic = TRUE) %>%
  pack_rows(outcome_order[4], 7, 8, bold = TRUE, italic = TRUE) %>%
  footnote(general = paste0(
             "Values shown as posterior mean (95% highest posterior density interval). ",
             "P(Direction): posterior probability that hard drug use worsens the outcome ",
             "(higher viral load or lower CD4+/QoL). ",
             "P(Clinically Meaningful): posterior probability that the effect exceeds ",
             "pre-specified thresholds (0.5 log10 VL, 50 CD4+ cells, 2 QoL points). ",
             "Priors: Normal(0, SD(outcome)) on coefficients; ",
             "half-Normal(0, 2*SD(outcome)) on sigma. ",
             "Reference group: no hard drug use."),
           general_title = "Note: ",
           footnote_as_chunk = TRUE)

table3_kbl


# =============================================================================
# TABLE 3b: BAYESIAN — ALL COVARIATE EFFECTS (Adjusted Model)
# =============================================================================
# Full posterior summary for all coefficients across 4 outcomes.
# Source: bayes_results (summary_adj tables) from bayes_results.RData

bayes_covar_table <- map_dfr(names(bayes_results), function(nm) {
  r <- bayes_results[[nm]]
  r$summary_adj %>%
    filter(!is.na(Variable), Variable != "sigma") %>%
    mutate(Outcome = r$label)
}) %>%
  mutate(
    Variable_label = var_labels[Variable],
    Variable = ifelse(is.na(Variable_label), Variable, Variable_label),
    cell     = paste0(
      formatC(Estimate, format = "f", digits = 3),
      " (",
      formatC(HPDI_2.5, format = "f", digits = 3),
      ", ",
      formatC(HPDI_97.5, format = "f", digits = 3),
      ")"
    ),
    Outcome = factor(Outcome, levels = outcome_order)
  ) %>%
  select(Outcome, Variable, cell) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Outcome, values_from = cell)

bayes_covar_table <- bayes_covar_table %>%
  mutate(Variable = factor(Variable, levels = var_display_order)) %>%
  arrange(Variable) %>%
  rename(" " = Variable)

table3b_kbl <- kbl(bayes_covar_table,
                   format  = "html",
                   align   = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c"),
                   caption = paste0("Table 3b. Bayesian Adjusted Model: ",
                                    "All Covariate Effects Across Outcomes"),
                   escape  = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE,
                font_size  = 12,
                position   = "center") %>%
  footnote(general = paste0(
             "Values shown as posterior mean (95% HPDI). ",
             "Model: change ~ hard drug use + age + race + education + BMI + smoking. ",
             "Outcomes are change scores (Year 2 minus Baseline). ",
             "Viral load modeled on the log10 scale. ",
             "Priors: Normal(0, SD(outcome)) on coefficients; ",
             "half-Normal(0, 2*SD(outcome)) on sigma."),
           general_title = "Note: ",
           footnote_as_chunk = TRUE)

table3b_kbl


# =============================================================================
# TABLE 4:  FREQUENTIST vs. BAYESIAN COMPARISON (Adjusted Model)
# =============================================================================
# Side-by-side point estimates, interval estimates, and inference

# Frequentist adjusted model
freq_adj <- drug_effects %>%
  filter(Model == "Adjusted") %>%
  mutate(
    Freq_Est = formatC(estimate, format = "f", digits = 3),
    Freq_CI  = paste0("(",
                      formatC(conf.low, format = "f", digits = 3),
                      ", ",
                      formatC(conf.high, format = "f", digits = 3),
                      ")"),
    Freq_p   = fmt_pval(p.value),
    Outcome  = factor(Outcome, levels = outcome_order)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Outcome) %>%
  select(Outcome, Freq_Est, Freq_CI, Freq_p)

# Bayesian adjusted model
bayes_adj <- bayes_drug_effects %>%
  filter(Model == "Adjusted") %>%
  left_join(
    posterior_probs %>% select(Outcome, P_direction),
    by = "Outcome"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Bayes_Est  = formatC(Estimate, format = "f", digits = 3),
    Bayes_CrI  = paste0("(",
                        formatC(HPDI_2.5, format = "f", digits = 3),
                        ", ",
                        formatC(HPDI_97.5, format = "f", digits = 3),
                        ")"),
    Bayes_Prob = formatC(P_direction, format = "f", digits = 3),
    Outcome    = factor(Outcome, levels = outcome_order)
  ) %>%
  arrange(Outcome) %>%
  select(Outcome, Bayes_Est, Bayes_CrI, Bayes_Prob)

# Merge
comparison_table <- freq_adj %>%
  left_join(bayes_adj, by = "Outcome") %>%
  mutate(Outcome = as.character(Outcome)) %>%
  rename(
    " "              = Outcome,
    "Estimate"       = Freq_Est,
    "95% CI"         = Freq_CI,
    "p-value"        = Freq_p,
    "Post. Mean"     = Bayes_Est,
    "95% HPDI"       = Bayes_CrI,
    "P(Direction)"   = Bayes_Prob
  )

table4_kbl <- kbl(comparison_table,
                  format  = "html",
                  align   = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c"),
                  caption = paste0("Table 4. Comparison of Frequentist and Bayesian ",
                                   "Estimates for the Effect of Hard Drug Use ",
                                   "(Adjusted Model)"),
                  escape  = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE,
                font_size  = 13,
                position   = "center") %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1,
                     "Frequentist" = 3,
                     "Bayesian" = 3)) %>%
  footnote(general = paste0(
             "Adjusted model: change ~ hard drug use + age + race + ",
             "education + BMI + smoking. ",
             "Frequentist: OLS with 95% confidence interval. ",
             "Bayesian: posterior mean with 95% highest posterior density interval. ",
             "P(Direction): posterior probability of effect in hypothesized direction."),
           general_title = "Note: ",
           footnote_as_chunk = TRUE)

table4_kbl


# =============================================================================
# TABLE 5:  MEDIATION ASSESSMENT — ADHERENCE
# =============================================================================
# Sources: mediation_compare (freq) and bayes_mediation (bayes)
# Shows attenuation of hard drug use coefficient when adherence is added

freq_med <- mediation_compare %>%
  mutate(
    Outcome      = factor(Outcome, levels = outcome_order),
    Freq_Adj     = formatC(est_adj, format = "f", digits = 3),
    Freq_Adj_Adh = formatC(est_adj_adh, format = "f", digits = 3),
    Freq_Change  = paste0(formatC(pct_change, format = "f", digits = 1), "%")
  ) %>%
  arrange(Outcome) %>%
  select(Outcome, Freq_Adj, Freq_Adj_Adh, Freq_Change)

bayes_med <- bayes_mediation %>%
  mutate(
    Outcome       = factor(Outcome, levels = outcome_order),
    Bayes_Adj     = formatC(est_adj, format = "f", digits = 3),
    Bayes_Adj_Adh = formatC(est_adj_adh, format = "f", digits = 3),
    Bayes_Change  = paste0(formatC(pct_change, format = "f", digits = 1), "%")
  ) %>%
  arrange(Outcome) %>%
  select(Outcome, Bayes_Adj, Bayes_Adj_Adh, Bayes_Change)

mediation_table <- freq_med %>%
  left_join(bayes_med, by = "Outcome") %>%
  mutate(Outcome = as.character(Outcome)) %>%
  rename(
    " "                    = Outcome,
    "Adjusted"             = Freq_Adj,
    "Adj + Adherence"      = Freq_Adj_Adh,
    "% Change"             = Freq_Change,
    "Adjusted "            = Bayes_Adj,
    "Adj + Adherence "     = Bayes_Adj_Adh,
    "% Change "            = Bayes_Change
  )

table5_kbl <- kbl(mediation_table,
                  format  = "html",
                  align   = c("l", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c", "c"),
                  caption = paste0("Table 5. Mediation Assessment: Attenuation of ",
                                   "Hard Drug Use Effect After Adding ART Adherence"),
                  escape  = FALSE) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"),
                full_width = FALSE,
                font_size  = 13,
                position   = "center") %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1,
                     "Frequentist" = 3,
                     "Bayesian" = 3)) %>%
  footnote(general = paste0(
             "Values are the estimated coefficient for hard drug use (Yes vs. No). ",
             "% Change = 100 * (Adj+Adherence - Adjusted) / Adjusted. ",
             "Attenuation toward zero when adherence is added suggests partial ",
             "mediation through treatment adherence."),
           general_title = "Note: ",
           footnote_as_chunk = TRUE)

table5_kbl



```
